rationale: Both correctly diagnose the None+1 crash on extended ratio tokens and confirm the same dit.yaml workaround credited to FayeSpica, matching the thread. Y more precisely mirrors the thread's actual causal narrative (PR #2713 removing the `modes` filter, forcing two-stage topology) and proposes a fail-fast guard that matches the thread's explicit 'fail fast with a clean error message' ask, whereas X omits PR #2713 from its final answer and instead proposes a speculative tolerant/graceful-degrade fix (silently emptying ratio slices) that isn't validated anywhere in the thread and assumes untested behavior. X also asserts specific YAML header text ('both MoE and DiT headers say Instruct') that reads as unverified/fabricated detail, a mild grounding risk not present in Y's version.
